title: anthemis alpina subsp. mucronulata
intro: Anthemis alpina subsp. mucronulata, also known as Alpine Chamomile, is a subspecies of the Anthemis alpina plant, which is a perennial herb that belongs to the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties, making it a popular remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Alpine Chamomile include its ability to calm digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote a good night's sleep, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In herbal preparations, Alpine Chamomile is often used as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
